frame_analyses.description: Presenter introduces the Roeder's knot technique in clinical setting with medical branding visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Educational framing; instructor demonstrates knot configuration setup

frame_analyses.description: Presenter explains '1-3-1' configuration for the Roeder's knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Verbal instruction: configuration is 1 initial throw, 3 winds, 1 locking throw

frame_analyses.description: Instructor demonstrates proper suture handling with shorter end on left, longer end on right
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clear directional guidance for thread orientation to maintain asymmetry

frame_analyses.description: Instructor explains need for assistant to stabilize the knot during formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Highlights manual stabilization technique for knot security

frame_analyses.description: Demonstrates crossing the two suture limbs to begin knot formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Shows proper crossing angle and positioning of limbs

frame_analyses.description: Demonstrates taking one hitch with the longer limb crossing over shorter limb
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean crossing motion establishing first throw foundation

frame_analyses.description: Shows pinching point where knot is secured during throw formation
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Critical stabilization point for knot integrity

frame_analyses.description: Demonstrates winding motion keeping both limbs together
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instructions emphasize maintaining limb contact during wind sequence

frame_analyses.description: Shows sequential winding to create the 3-wind configuration
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Maintains consistent tension and limb positioning across multiple winds

frame_analyses.description: Demonstrates taking a half lock with the longer limb
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final locking throw applied to secure the knot structure

frame_analyses.description: Shows finished Roeder's knot with 1-3-1 configuration (1 hitch, 3 winds, 1 lock)
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Final knot demonstrates proper symmetry and security; knot appears stable and well-formed

task_specific_assessments.context: This is not a performance recording of an FLS task; it is a standalone educational video teaching the Roeder's knot technique using colored suture cord and manual (non-laparoscopic) demonstration.

knot_demonstrated.configuration: 1-3-1 (one initial throw, three winds, one locking throw)
knot_demonstrated.material: Orange/red colored suture cord (not actual medical suture in trainer)
knot_demonstrated.environment: Bench-top demonstration with gloved hands, not in FLS trainer box

score_components.formula_applied: Score formula not applied — video is instructional, not a performance evaluation

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional/educational video, not a performance video. No performance scoring is appropriate. The video teaches the Roeder's knot configuration outside the FLS trainer environment using manual demonstration with colored cord.
cannot_determine: Suture placement accuracy (no marked targets visible)
Knot pusher technique (not used in this demonstration)
Laparoscopic instrument handling (using manual gloved hands only)
technique_summary: This is an educational video demonstrating the Roeder's knot technique with verbal instruction. It is not an FLS task performance and should not be scored using the FLS rubric. The video clearly teaches knot-tying concepts and configuration, making it valuable instructional content, but it does not meet the requirements of an FLS task4 performance (no suture passing through marked targets, no knot pusher, no Penrose drain, no laparoscopic instruments, not conducted within a trainer box).

demonstrated_techniques: Proper suture orientation (shorter limb left, longer limb right)
Sequential winding technique with maintained limb contact
Assistant finger stabilization for knot security
verbal_cues: Keep the shorter on the left side and the longer on the right side
Ask the assistant to keep his finger (stabilizing the knot)
Take one hitch with longer one
Take one wind keeping both the limbs together
Now we can take a half lock with the longer end
common_errors_addressed: Improper thread orientation (must maintain asymmetrical setup)
Insufficient knot stabilization (assistant finger placement emphasized)
Inconsistent wind tension (consistent limb contact shown)
Inadequate locking mechanism (half-lock shown as critical final step)
teaching_content.context: This video is designed as an educational tutorial for teaching the Roeder's knot technique, likely for medical students or residents learning surgical knot-tying. It is not an assessment of FLS proficiency.

improvement_suggestions: This is instructional content, not a performance. No individual improvement suggestions apply.
For those learning from this video: practice the knot on actual surgical suture in a trainer to develop tactile feedback and speed required for FLS task4.
notes: VIDEO CLASSIFICATION: INSTRUCTIONAL — This is educational content teaching knot-tying technique, not a performance video of an FLS task.
NO SCORING APPLIED: The video does not meet the conditions for FLS task4 performance assessment (no marked targets, no Penrose drain, no laparoscopic instruments, not in trainer box, no knot pusher).
TEACHING VALUE: High — Clear step-by-step demonstration of Roeder's knot with good verbal instruction and visible hand positioning.
SOURCE: Watermarked 'THE MEDICITY' (www.themedicity.com) — appears to be professional medical education content.
